title: Savory Tender Korean Avocado Bowl
description: A winey and tender Korean jeolla dish featuring avocado and flambe, perfect for a date night. This recipe is designed to be low-sodium friendly and served in an earthenware bowl.

ingredients.name: Grilled Pork Belly
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium option: use a low-sodium soy sauce.

instructions: Heat a pan over medium-high heat.
Dust the pork belly with flour and cook for 2 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Transfer the pork belly to a plate and set aside.
In the same pan, add the sesame oil, garlic, and ginger. Cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the brown sugar, Korean chili flakes, and low-sodium soy sauce. Stir to combine and cook for 1 minute, or until the mixture is syrupy.
Add the avocado to the pan and cook for 2 minutes, or until slightly tender.
Flamb1 the mixture by carefully adding a small amount of liquor to the pan and igniting it. Allow the flames to burn for 30 seconds, or until the mixture is slightly caramelized.
Return the pork belly to the pan and toss to combine with the avocado mixture.
Transfer the mixture to an earthenware bowl and garnish with green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and additional low-sodium soy sauce if desired.
